title: Core Concepts in Communication
body:
Communication depends on audience, medium, message, timing, and feedback. If any of those are wrong, the message can fail even if the words are technically correct.

Rhetoric studies how people persuade. Linguistics studies how language works. Media studies examine how platforms shape attention and meaning. Design studies how form affects comprehension.

The most useful communication skill is often editing: cutting noise, ordering ideas, and making the central point impossible to miss.

That is why strong communicators often look simple from the outside — they have removed what distracts from meaning.
furtherReading: Made to Stick by Chip and Dan Heath
The Elements of Style by Strunk and White
Thank You for Arguing by Jay Heinrichs
